edges.rationale: Hume interrupted Kant's dogmatic slumber (Prolegomena preface).
Kant was trained in the Leibniz-Wolffian rationalist school.
The movement (Fichte, Schelling, Hegel) grew out of the critical philosophy.
Schopenhauer built his system on Kant's transcendental idealism.
Schopenhauer as Educator; Nietzsche's early philosophy is Schopenhauerian.
Mill was raised within Bentham's circle as his intellectual heir.
Sidgwick's utilitarianism develops out of Mill's (Methods preface).
Singer is explicitly Sidgwickian (The Point of View of the Universe).
Parfit regarded Sidgwick's Methods as the best book on ethics.
Moore attended Sidgwick's Cambridge lectures; Principia engages him throughout.
Ross acknowledges Prichard's influence; both Oxford intuitionists.
Socrates is Plato's teacher and dialogical protagonist.
Twenty years in Plato's Academy.
Aquinas synthesised Aristotle ('the Philosopher') with Christian theology.
Hume's moral sentimentalism develops Hutcheson's moral sense theory.
Ayer presented logical positivism as the heir of Hume's empiricism.
Blackburn's projectivism is self-described neo-Humeanism.
Korsgaard is the leading contemporary Kantian constructivist.
Justice as fairness is presented as Kantian constructivism.
Scanlon's contractualism develops within the Rawlsian contract tradition.
After Virtue defends an Aristotelian tradition-based ethics.
Her virtue ethics is explicitly neo-Aristotelian.
Zeno founded the Stoa around 300 BCE.
Epicurus founded the Garden and its school.
The rival Hellenistic schools: virtue as sole good vs pleasure as the good.
The classic early modern opposition over the sources of knowledge.
High scholasticism built its method and ethics on recovered Aristotle.
Williams's integrity objection (Smart & Williams 1973).
Butler's Fifteen Sermons contain the classic refutation of psychological egoism.
Foot's 'Moral Beliefs' and related papers attack Hare's prescriptivism.

proposed_nodes.label: G. W. F. Hegel
proposed_nodes.reason: Central German idealist; needed to flesh out the post-Kantian lineage.

proposed_nodes.label: Fifteen Sermons
proposed_nodes.reason: Butler 1726; carries his refutation of psychological egoism.
